sampādako.meaning: one who accomplishes, who brings about; (in the negative context cited) one who does not obtain
breakdown: Root meaning “to go, attain”; causative sampādeti: to accomplish, procure.
sampādako.note: uncertain: the lexicon's note reads “not attain: he does not obtain”, glossing a negated context (asampādako); the word itself means “one who accomplishes/procures”.

sampādetha.summary: Famous from the Buddha's last words: appamādena sampādetha — “strive on diligently”.
sampādetha.gram: verb, imperative 2nd person plural (causative of sampajjati, √pad)

sampāpaka.meaning: leading to; causing to reach or obtain
breakdown: Agent/adjectival suffix: that which causes to reach.

samucchindissanti.meaning: they will break off completely; they will extirpate
samucchindissanti.gram: verb, future 3rd person plural of samucchindati (saṁ + ud + √chid)
breakdown: Root meaning “to cut”. Together: to cut off entirely, uproot.

samudayasaccaniddeso.meaning: the exposition of the truth of arising (of suffering)

samuddakasuttaṁ.gram: proper noun, neuter (title of a discourse)

samuddharaṇāyā’ti.meaning: “…for the pulling out, the lifting up” (end of quotation)
samuddharaṇāyā’ti.gram: feminine action noun (dative singular) + quotation particle ti
breakdown: Dative singular ending: “for the sake of”.
Quotation-closing particle (iti), fused with the lengthened final vowel.

samudācaratha.meaning: you should behave towards; you should address
samudācaratha.gram: verb, 2nd person plural (imperative/optative) of samudācarati (saṁ + ud + ā + √car)
breakdown: Root “to move, conduct oneself”: to behave towards, address.

samudācarissati.meaning: will occur to; will befall; will be current
samudācarissati.gram: verb, future 3rd person singular of samudācarati
breakdown: Root “to move”: to occur, arise habitually.

samuggacchati.gram: verb, present tense 3rd person singular (saṁ + ud + √gam)

samugghāṭayamānā.gram: present participle (middle voice, nominative plural) of samugghāṭeti

samuppannānaṁ.meaning: of (things) arisen; of what has been produced
breakdown: Root “to go” with past participle suffix: arisen, produced (uppanna).

samusseyya.meaning: should raise up; would lift up
samusseyya.gram: verb, optative 3rd person singular of samusseti (saṁ + ud + √si)

samuttejetvā.meaning: having inspired; having excited, stirred, filled with delight
breakdown: Root “to sharpen”: causative, to incite, inspire.

samācare.meaning: should perform; would practise, conduct oneself
samācare.gram: verb, optative 3rd person singular of samācarati (saṁ + ā + √car)
breakdown: Root “to move, conduct”: to behave, perform.

samādapeti.meaning: causes to take up; incites, encourages (in the good)
samādapeti.gram: verb, present tense 3rd person singular (causative of samādiyati, √ādā)

samādhi.meaning: concentration; mental concentration; collectedness of mind
samādhi.summary: The second of the three trainings of the path, between sīla and paññā.
samādhi.gram: masculine abstract noun, from samādahati (saṁ + ā + √dhā)
breakdown: Root meaning “to place, put”. Samādhi: placing the mind together, collectedness.
samādhi.goenka: Samādhi is concentration — gaining control of the mind through the practice of mental concentration. It is the second of the three trainings of the path: established in sīla (morality), the meditator develops samādhi, mastery over the mind, which becomes the base for paññā, experiential wisdom. In this tradition samādhi is cultivated through ānāpāna, observing the natural breath. The volumes describe accomplished meditators as “firmly fixed in concentration” (thirasamādhiyuttena cetasā), which proves their accomplishment in samādhi.

samādhijaṁ.meaning: born of concentration; arising from samādhi

samādhimānantarikaññamāhu.meaning: “and the concentration which they call immediate (in result)”
samādhimānantarikaññamāhu.gram: sandhi fusion: noun + adjective + pronoun + verb (samādhiṁ + ānantarikaṁ + yaṁ + āhu)
breakdown: Immediately following, bearing immediate fruit (from anantara “without interval”).
Which (relative pronoun; yaṁ + … gives -aññam- in sandhi).
They call, they have declared (perfect of √ah).
Root “to take” with past participle suffix: taken up, undertaken.

samādinnena.meaning: by what has been undertaken; through what is taken upon oneself

breakdown: Root “to take” with past participle suffix: taken up.

samādiyitabbaṁ.meaning: to be undertaken; that should be taken upon oneself
samādiyitabbaṁ.gram: potential participle of samādiyati (nominative/accusative singular neuter)

samādānahetu.meaning: by reason of undertaking; because of taking up (a practice or vow)
breakdown: Cause, reason; at the end of a compound: “by reason of”.

samādāya.meaning: having undertaken; acting in accordance with; having taken up

samādāyeva.meaning: having just undertaken; taking up precisely (these rules)
samādāyeva.gram: gerund of samādāti + emphatic particle eva (sandhi fusion)
breakdown: Just, only (emphatic enclitic; the final -a and e- merge in sandhi).

samāgacchati.gram: verb, present tense 3rd person singular (saṁ + ā + √gam)

breakdown: Root “to come” with noun suffix: a coming together.
Coming. Together: a coming together, union.

samāgatāni.gram: past participle of samāgacchati (nominative/accusative plural neuter)
breakdown: Come (past participle of āgacchati). Together: come together, gathered.

samānasukhadukkho.meaning: sharing equally in happiness and sorrow; an equal in joy and pain (said of a true friend)

samāpajjati.meaning: enters upon; attains to (a meditative state); undergoes
samāpajjati.gram: verb, present tense 3rd person singular (saṁ + ā + √pad)
breakdown: Goes to (√pad). Together: enters upon, attains.
Root “to go, attain” with abstract suffix: attainment, entering upon.
Root meaning “to strike”: to remove, abolish.

samūhanti.gram: verb, present tense 3rd person singular of samūhanati
breakdown: Root meaning “to strike”: to remove, uproot.
Root “to strike” with past participle suffix: struck out, abolished.

sandassetvā.meaning: having instructed; having pointed out, taught, explained

sandhi.meaning: union; junction; joining (of words or things)
sandhi.summary: As a grammatical term, sandhi names the sound changes where words join.
breakdown: Root meaning “to put, join”. Literally: a putting together.

sandhiyatimeva.meaning: is joined together thus; is just being connected
sandhiyatimeva.gram: verb, present passive 3rd person singular of sandahati + emphatic eva
breakdown: Is put together, is joined (passive of sandahati, saṁ + √dhā).

sandissamānaṁ.meaning: being seen together; agreeing with, corresponding (used of comparing and verifying)

sandississanti.meaning: they will be seen (together); will be found, will appear
sandississanti.gram: verb, future 3rd person plural of sandissati (passive of saṁ + √dis)

sandiṭṭhiko.meaning: to be experienced directly; visible here and now
sandiṭṭhiko.summary: One of the classic epithets of the Dhamma: its results are seen for oneself in this very life.
breakdown: Adjectival suffix: belonging to what is seen.
sandiṭṭhiko.goenka: Sandiṭṭhiko is one of the qualities of the Dhamma recited in the traditional formula: the Dhamma is to be experienced directly, visible here and now. The teaching is not to be accepted on faith or speculation about the future; the meditator verifies it personally, within the framework of his own body, in this very life. Each step of the path gives its result here and now, to be seen for oneself.

breakdown: Root “to go” with noun suffix: a coming together.
saṅgama.note: uncertain: the headword appears with stripped diacritics in the source glossary; the standard IAST form is saṅgama.

saṅkhaṅgata.meaning: is called; is reckoned as, designated
saṅkhaṅgata.gram: adjective/past participle compound (saṅkhaṁ gata “gone to the reckoning of”)
breakdown: Gone (past participle of gacchati). Together: come to be reckoned as, called.
saṅkhaṅgata.note: uncertain: the headword appears with stripped diacritics in the source glossary; the standard IAST form is saṅkhaṅgata (saṅkhaṁ gata).

sanniggaṇhāmi.meaning: I restrain; I hold in check
sanniggaṇhāmi.gram: verb, present tense 1st person singular (saṁ + niggaṇhāti)

sannipātā.gram: masculine noun (nominative plural / ablative singular), from sannipatati
breakdown: Root “to fall, alight” with noun suffix: a falling together, assembly.
